input.contents:
# Elay System Requirements — Calendar, Meeting Minutes, Task Management

> **Project:** Elay Backend — `Customer/Api`  
> **Version:** 1.0 (draft)  
> **Scope:** Backend logic only. Frontend is implemented by a separate team.

This document is the **master requirements spec** for three integrated modules. Detailed Calendar API design lives in `Calendar/01-Calendar-Overview.md` and `Calendar/02-Calendar-Scenarios.md`.

---

## 1. Executive summary

Three new modules will be implemented in the Customer API layer. They are integrated and cover a full organizational workflow cycle:

| Module | Current state | Goal |
|---|---|---|
| **Enterprise Calendar** | Does not exist | Internal scheduling similar to Google Calendar, scoped to the organization |
| **Meeting Minutes** | Does not exist | Structured meeting documentation and action-item tracking |
| **Task Management** | Partially implemented | Complete organizational task system |

---

## 2. Enterprise Calendar module

### 2.1 Vision

An internal scheduling tool similar to Google Calendar, fully scoped to the organization. This spec covers **backend logic only** (`Customer/Api`).

### 2.2 Calendar types

Each user can access three calendar layers:

- **Personal** — owned by the user; private events appear as **busy only** to others
- **Unit** — shared events for a department/team
- **Organization** — company-wide public events

### 2.3 V1 capabilities

**Event management**
- Create events with title, description, location, start/end
- All-day events (leave, company holidays)
- Six event types with colors: Urgent / Private / Confirmed / Meeting / Info / Deadline
- File attachments (max 10 MB)
- Personal notes on events
- Soft delete

**Invites & participation**
- Invite internal users
- Invite external guests by mobile number (optional / backlog)
- RSVP: Accept / Decline / Maybe
- View participant list before accepting
- Star favorite contacts for quick invite

**Free/Busy & meeting request (differentiator)**
- Any user can see others’ calendars as **free/busy slots only** (no title/details)
- Meeting request flow: pick a free slot → send request (not a confirmed event) → target user notified → approve/reject
- On approve: event created for both + 15-minute reminder (in-app + SMS)
- On reject: notification to requester
- Event status: `Pending` → `Confirmed` / `Rejected` / `Cancelled`

**Recurrence (V1)**
- Daily / weekly / monthly / yearly / custom
- Edit scope: this occurrence / this and following / entire series

**Reminders**
- User-configurable offset and channel
- Channels: in-app notification + SMS
- Auto 15-minute reminder for confirmed meetings

**Integration**
- Show task due dates on calendar (read-only, `TaskDeadline`)
- Link events to messages (`Message`) — later phase
- Link events to tasks (`TaskItem`) — later phase
- Auto follow-up task after minutes completion — later phase

**Manager view**
- Manager sees subordinate events via `OrgPosition` tree
- Subordinate personal events: **busy only**, no title
- Separate company holiday/leave calendar

### 2.4 Calendar database entities (summary)

| Entity | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Calendar` | Personal / unit / organization calendar |
| `CalendarEvent` | Event with status Pending/Confirmed/Rejected/Cancelled |
| `CalendarParticipant` | Invitees + RSVP status |
| `CalendarReminder` | Reminders (offset + InApp/Sms) |
| `CalendarRecurrence` | Custom recurrence rule |
| `CalendarAttachment` | Event file attachments (max 10 MB) |
| `CalendarEventNote` | Personal note on event |
| `MeetingRequest` | Approval-based meeting request flow |
| `FavoriteContact` | Starred contacts for quick invite |

---

## 3. Meeting Minutes module

### 3.1 Vision

A structured form linked to each calendar meeting. Includes agenda, decisions, assignee tasks, outcome, and approvals.

### 3.2 Lifecycle

1. Meeting created in calendar  
2. Minutes form completed (agenda, topics, decisions)  
3. Action items assigned to owners  
4. Notification sent to each assignee for task acceptance  
5. Accepted tasks move to assignee’s personal task list  
6. Completion status tracked back in the minutes document  

### 3.3 Core capabilities

**Minutes content**
- Agenda
- Attendees: internal users + external guests (mobile)
- Discussion topics
- Decisions / resolutions
- Overall meeting outcome
- Private personal note per participant

**Assignee task management**
- Assign task with due date
- Notify assignee
- Assignee accepts or rejects task
- Accepted task auto-added to assignee’s task list
- Completion status in minutes: done / not done + explanation
- Completion timestamp recorded in minutes when marked done

**External participants**
- Add guest by name + mobile
- SMS summary of tasks to guest
- Guest account / portal (backlog)
- Guest task confirmation via mobile/portal

**Follow-up tasks**
- Auto create follow-up task after minutes completion
- Follow-up appears on calendar

**Approvals**
- Digital sign-off request from participants
- Per-participant approval status visible

### 3.4 Minutes entities (summary)

| Entity | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `MeetingMinutes` | Main minutes record (linked to `CalendarEvent`) |
| `MinutesAgendaItem` | Agenda items |
| `MinutesDecision` | Decisions |
| `MinutesAction` | Assigned action items |
| `MinutesActionStatus` | Completion status per action |
| `MinutesParticipant` | Internal + guest participants |
| `MinutesPersonalNote` | Private note per participant |
| `MinutesApproval` | Digital approvals |

### 3.5 Backlog ideas

- Version history for minutes edits (V2)
- PDF export with org template (V2)
- Minutes templates by meeting type (V2)
- Search across all minutes (V1)
- Action-item completion analytics (V3)
- Re-notification for near-due assignee tasks (V2)

---

## 4. Task Management module

### 4.1 Current state

Task module exists but is incomplete. Goal: complete existing features and add new ones.

### 4.2 Required capabilities

**Structure**
- Task folders for categorization
- Personal task space (separate from org tasks)
- Move task from personal to org folder
- To Do / Done split

**Task details**
- Title, description
- Start/end time
- Due date
- Reminder with notification
- Priority
- Tags

**Integration**
- Minutes-derived tasks auto-appear in assignee list
- Due dates visible on calendar (read-only)
- Follow-up tasks after meetings
- Task completion status reflected in minutes
- Reason required when not completed

### 4.3 Backlog ideas

- Recurring tasks (V2)
- Subtasks (V2)
- Productivity reports (V3)
- Task sharing with audit trail (V2)
- Link to Message or Project (V1)
- Quick add task from calendar range (V1)

---

## 5. Shared infrastructure

### 5.1 SMS service

SMS does not exist today. Build from scratch:

- `ISmsService` with `SendAsync(phone, text)`
- Initial implementation: **logger/mock** until provider is chosen
- Config in `appsettings`: key, sender id, URL
- Candidate providers: Melipayamak, Faraz, Kavenegar (decision later)

### 5.2 Reminder background service

Similar to existing `SyncBackgroundService`:

- `ReminderBackgroundService` runs every minute
- Finds reminders due (`StartAt - OffsetMinutes <= now`, `IsSent = false`)
- Sends in-app notification via `NotificationCenter`
- Sends SMS via `ISmsService`
- Marks sent to prevent duplicates

### 5.3 In-app notifications

Use existing `NotificationCenter` for:

- Meeting invites
- Meeting request approve/reject
- Pre-meeting reminders
- Minutes action-item assignment
- Meeting request rejection to requester

---

## 6. Proposed implementation phases (all modules)

| Phase | Focus | Key output |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Enums + error classes | Shared types, no public API |
| 1 | SMS infrastructure | `ISmsService` + mock |
| 2 | Calendar core | CRUD + range query ⚠️ migration |
| 3 | Participants | Invites + RSVP ⚠️ migration |
| 4 | Recurrence | Custom recurrence + edit scopes ⚠️ migration |
| 5 | Reminders + meeting request | Reminder tables + background job ⚠️ migration |
| 6 | Free/Busy | Free/busy + unified range with task deadlines |
| 7 | Meeting minutes | Minutes entities + APIs ⚠️ migration |
| 8 | Task completion | Folders, personal space, full integration ⚠️ migration |

⚠️ = human-reviewed EF migration required

---

## 7. Cross-module integration map

| From | To | Relationship |
|---|---|---|
| Calendar | Meeting Minutes | Meeting events can have minutes |
| Meeting Minutes | Task | Action items become assignee tasks |
| Task | Calendar | Due dates shown on calendar (read-only) |
| Task | Meeting Minutes | Completion status flows back to minutes |
| Calendar | Task | Quick-create task from calendar slot |
| Meeting Minutes | Calendar | Follow-up task → new calendar event |

---

## 8. Non-functional requirements

- All successful API responses: **HTTP 200** (project convention)
- JWT auth required on all endpoints
- Rate limiting: `RateLimitConst.RateFix`
- Soft delete on all entities
- Jalali dates in UI only; API uses standard `DateTime` (UTC)
- `CancellationToken` on all async operations
- XML docs on public members
- Max attachment size: 10 MB
- In-app notifications via existing `NotificationCenter`

---

## 9. Out of scope for V1

- Google Calendar / Outlook sync
- Public calendar share links
- Integrated video conferencing
- Complex room booking (backlog V3)
- iCal export
- Advanced analytics dashboards
- Multi-language UI

---

## 10. Related documents

| Document | Content |
|---|---|
| `Calendar/01-Calendar-Overview.md` | Calendar design contract, entities, ERD, API list, phasing |
| `Calendar/02-Calendar-Scenarios.md` | Per-endpoint scenarios, inputs, status codes, expected outputs |

---

## Sign-off (draft)

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product Owner | | | |
| System Architect | | | |
| Implementation Lead | | | |

# Elay Enterprise Calendar — Design Document (File 1: Overview)

> This document is the **design contract** for the Enterprise Calendar feature in `Customer/Api`.  
> Backend logic only; frontend is out of scope.  
> **Not an implementation spec** — no code is written until final approval.

Table of contents:
1. [Feature summary](#1-feature-summary)
2. [Decisions locked in](#2-decisions-locked-in)
3. [Phasing](#3-phasing)
4. [New entities + relationships](#4-new-entities--relationships)
5. [Enums and six event types](#5-enums-and-six-event-types)
6. [ERD (Mermaid)](#6-erd-mermaid)
7. [Full API list](#7-full-api-list)
8. [Meeting backlog ideas](#8-meeting-backlog-ideas)
9. [Shared API conventions](#9-shared-api-conventions)

---

## 1. Feature summary

A Google Calendar–like experience **inside the organization**, with these differentiators:

- **Free/Busy:** users see others’ busy slots without any details (no title/description/participants).
- **Approval-based meeting request:** request → approve/reject → confirmed event for both parties.
- **Reminders:** in-app notification + SMS (V1), user-configurable offset.
- **Custom recurrence** from V1 + series edit with three scopes.
- **Manager view:** subordinate busy slots via `OrgPosition` tree.
- **Task due dates on calendar** (read-only).
- **File attachments + notes on events** (files up to 10 MB).

---

## 2. Decisions locked in

| Topic | Decision |
|---|---|
| Target users | All employees |
| Invites | Select users from org directory |
| Jalali dates | UI only; API uses standard `DateTime` (UTC) |
| Recurrence | V1, custom rules supported |
| Series edit scope | All three: this only / this and following / entire series |
| Personal events in Free/Busy | Show as **busy only** (no title) |
| Meeting request | Approval flow + reject notification to requester |
| Reminders | User-defined + auto 15 min before confirmed meetings |
| SMS | Build `ISmsService` + logger implementation first; real provider later |
| Time conflict | **Block** with message “user is in a meeting” |
| Attachments | Max 10 MB; allowed: image / PDF / Word |
| Personal calendar creation | Auto-created on first use |
| `Range` query max span | 92 days |
| Cancel meeting request | Yes (E7) |
| Out of Calendar V1 | Google/Outlook sync, public links, iCal, advanced reports |
| External guests | Documented as optional/backlog until product final sign-off |
| Room/resource booking | Backlog |
| Smart Slot Finder | Backlog |
| Smart buffer between meetings | Backlog |
| Scheduling links | Backlog |
| Company holiday calendar | Separate system calendar (can ship without external integrations) |

---

## 3. Phasing

⚠️ = requires EF migration (human-reviewed).

| Phase | Title | Migration | API |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Foundations: enums + error classes | ⚠️ | — |
| 1 | SMS infrastructure (`ISmsService` + logger) | — | — |
| 2 | Calendar/event core (CRUD + range) | ⚠️ | A1–A4, B1–B5 |
| 3 | Recurrence + series exceptions | ⚠️ | B2/B3 (scope), B5 (expand) |
| 4 | Invites + RSVP | ⚠️ | D1–D4 |
| 5 | Free/Busy + subordinate view | — | C1, B6 |
| 6 | Meeting request (approval flow) | ⚠️ | E1–E7 |
| 7 | Reminders + `ReminderBackgroundService` | ⚠️ | F1–F3 |
| 8 | Attachments + notes | ⚠️ | G1–G6 |
| 9 | Task deadlines on range (read-only) | — | extend B5 |
| 10 | Standardize conflict blocking (409) | — | B1/B2/D1/E1/E2 |
| 11 | Favorite contacts + company holidays | ⚠️ | H1–H4 |
| 12 | Smart Slot Finder + meeting buffer | — | I1–I2 |
| 13 | Room/resource booking | ⚠️ | J1–J5 |
| 14 | Scheduling links + guests (optional) | ⚠️ | K1–K6 |

---

## 4. New entities + relationships

### Core calendar entities
- `Calendar` (Personal / Unit / Organization)
- `CalendarEvent` (`EventStatus`: Pending / Confirmed / Rejected / Cancelled)
- `CalendarParticipant` (invitees + RSVP)
- `CalendarReminder` (offset + channel)
- `CalendarRecurrence` (recurrence rule)
- `CalendarEventException` (single occurrence override in a series)
- `CalendarAttachment` (file attachment)
- `CalendarEventNote` (note)
- `MeetingRequest` (approval-based meeting request)

### Added from meeting notes (backlog)
- `FavoriteContact` (starred invite contact)
- `CalendarHoliday` (company holiday/leave)
- `CalendarResource` + `CalendarResourceBooking` (room/equipment)
- `CalendarSchedulingLink` (scheduling link)
- `CalendarUserPreference` (default buffer minutes)
- `GuestContact` (external guest — optional)

### Links to existing entities
- `AppUser` ↔ events, invites, meeting requests, notes, preferences
- `OrgPosition` for unit calendars and manager subordinate view
- `TaskItem` for read-only `DueDate` on range queries
- `NotificationCenter` for request/approve/reminder notifications at implementation time

---

## 5. Enums and six event types

```
EventType            = Urgent, Private, Confirmed, Meeting, Info, Deadline
CalendarType         = Personal, Unit, Organization
EventStatus          = Pending, Confirmed, Rejected, Cancelled
ParticipantResponse  = Pending, Accepted, Declined, Maybe
ReminderChannel      = InApp, Sms
RecurrenceType       = None, Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly, Custom
RecurrenceEditScope  = ThisOnly, ThisAndFollowing, All
MeetingRequestStatus = Pending, Approved, Rejected, Cancelled
CalendarHolidayScope = Organization, Unit, Project
CalendarResourceType = Room, Equipment, Other
```

### Six event types (UI color icons)

| Type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `Urgent` | Alert / urgent |
| `Private` | Confidential |
| `Confirmed` | Confirmed / final |
| `Meeting` | Meeting / appointment |
| `Info` | Informational |
| `Deadline` | Scheduling / deadline |

> Add a new value to existing `NotificationType` (e.g. `Calendar` or `Meeting`) for calendar notifications.

## 9. Shared API conventions

- All endpoints: `RequireAuthorization()` + `RequireRateLimiting(RateLimitConst.RateFix)`.
- Current user always from token (`ICurrentUserService.UserId()`), never from request body.
- Dates: standard `DateTime` (UTC / ISO 8601). Jalali conversion is frontend responsibility.
- Success: `200 OK`, body = raw value (`Results.Ok(...)`).
- Errors: `ProblemDetails` (400/403/404/409); 401/429 with no body.
- Business error text in `Error.Description` may be **Persian at runtime** per project convention; this doc uses English for clarity.

# Elay Enterprise Calendar — Scenario Document (File 2)

> For **each API**, this file describes all request outcomes: valid input, validation rules, every error status code, and **exact expected response body**.  
> Entity and enum reference: `01-Calendar-Overview.md`.

## How to read this document

Each endpoint includes:
- **Valid input:** parameters + rules.
- **Scenario table:** one row per case = condition + status code + expected body.

### Success response (200)
Body = raw value (no wrapper). Examples show expected JSON.

### Error response (400/403/404/409)
Standard ASP.NET `ProblemDetails`:
```json
{
  "type": "https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1",
  "title": "One or more errors occurred.",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "<Error.Description — Persian at runtime per project convention>"
}
```
- **401:** no body (Authorization middleware).
- **429:** no body (RateLimiter; may include `Retry-After` header).

> For 401/429, tables show “no body”. These apply to **all** endpoints and are not repeated per endpoint.

---

# Group A — Calendars

## A1. GET `/Calendar/List`

**Valid input:** none (user from token).

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  { "id": 1, "title": "My calendar", "type": "Personal", "ownerUserId": 42, "orgPositionId": null, "color": "#2196F3", "canWrite": true },
  { "id": 7, "title": "Finance unit", "type": "Unit", "ownerUserId": null, "orgPositionId": 5, "color": "#4CAF50", "canWrite": true },
  { "id": 9, "title": "Organization", "type": "Organization", "ownerUserId": null, "orgPositionId": null, "color": "#9E9E9E", "canWrite": false }
]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1-1 | Valid user, no personal calendar yet | 200 | Array; personal calendar **auto-created and returned** |
| A1-2 | Valid user with multiple calendars | 200 | Full calendar array |
| A1-3 | No token | 401 | No body |
| A1-4 | Rate limit exceeded | 429 | No body |

---

## A2. POST `/Calendar`

**Valid input — `CalendarCreateDto`:**
| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `title` | Required, length 2..200 |
| `type` | Required, one of Personal/Unit/Organization |
| `orgPositionId` | Required when `type=Unit`; must be existing position |
| `color` | Optional; default if empty |

**200 output:** `int` (created calendar id). Example: `12`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| A2-1 | Valid complete input | 200 | `12` |
| A2-2 | Empty `title` | 400 | ProblemDetails: calendar title required |
| A2-3 | `title` shorter than 2 | 400 | ProblemDetails: title min 2 characters |
| A2-4 | `type=Unit` but missing `orgPositionId` | 400 | ProblemDetails: org position required for unit calendar |
| A2-5 | `orgPositionId` points to missing position | 404 | ProblemDetails: org position not found |
| A2-6 | `type=Organization` but user not allowed | 403 | ProblemDetails: not allowed to create org calendar |
| A2-7 | Unexpected server error | 400 | ProblemDetails: `ServerError` |

---

## A3. PUT `/Calendar`

**Valid input — `CalendarEditDto`:** `{ id:int, title:string(2..200), color:string }`

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| A3-1 | Valid, user owner/authorized | 200 | `true` |
| A3-2 | Invalid `title` | 400 | ProblemDetails: validation message |
| A3-3 | Calendar missing or soft-deleted | 404 | ProblemDetails: calendar not found |
| A3-4 | User not allowed to edit | 403 | ProblemDetails: not allowed to edit this calendar |

---

## A4. DELETE `/Calendar/{id}`

**Valid input:** `id` in route.  
**Behavior:** soft delete; child events become inaccessible.  
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| A4-1 | Calendar exists, user authorized | 200 | `true` |
| A4-2 | Calendar missing/deleted | 404 | ProblemDetails: calendar not found |
| A4-3 | User not authorized | 403 | ProblemDetails: not allowed to delete this calendar |
| A4-4 | Default personal calendar (must not delete) | 400 | ProblemDetails: default personal calendar cannot be deleted |

---

# Group B — Events

## B1. POST `/Calendar/Event`

**Valid input — `EventCreateDto`:**
| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `calendarId` | Required; calendar exists and user can write |
| `title` | Required, 2..200 |
| `description` | Optional, max 2048 |
| `type` | Required, from `EventType` |
| `location` | Optional, free text |
| `startAt` | Required |
| `endAt` | Required, must be `> startAt` |
| `isAllDay` | Default false |
| `isPersonal` | Default false |
| `visibleOnlyToParticipants` | Default false |
| `participantUserIds` | Optional; all must be valid users |
| `reminders[]` | Optional; each `offsetMinutes >= 0`, valid `channel` |
| `recurrence` | Optional; if present must be valid (see B-Recur) |

**200 output:** `int` (event id). Example: `501`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| B1-1 | Valid input, no conflict | 200 | `501` |
| B1-2 | Empty `title` | 400 | Event title required |
| B1-3 | `endAt <= startAt` | 400 | End time must be after start time |
| B1-4 | `description` > 2048 | 400 | Description max 2048 characters |
| B1-5 | Invalid `type` | 400 | Invalid event type |
| B1-6 | `calendarId` not found | 404 | Calendar not found |
| B1-7 | User cannot write to calendar | 403 | Not allowed to create event in this calendar |
| B1-8 | Invalid `participantUserIds` entry | 404 | Invited user not found |
| B1-9 | Invitee has Confirmed event in range | 409 | User {name} is in a meeting in that time range |
| B1-10 | Negative `reminder.offsetMinutes` | 400 | Invalid reminder offset |
| B1-11 | Unexpected error | 400 | `ServerError` |

**Recurrence rules (B-Recur):** valid `type`; `interval >= 1`; for Weekly at least one `weekDays` entry; exactly one of `endDate` or `occurrenceCount` (not both, not neither) — else **400** with matching message.

---

## B2. PUT `/Calendar/Event`

**Valid input — `EventEditDto`:** all B1 fields + `id:int` + `editScope` + `occurrenceDate?`  
- `editScope` only for recurring events: `ThisOnly` / `ThisAndFollowing` / `All`.  
- `occurrenceDate` required when `editScope != All`.

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| B2-1 | Non-recurring, valid edit | 200 | `true` |
| B2-2 | Recurring, `editScope=ThisOnly` | 200 | `true` (exception/override created) |
| B2-3 | Recurring, `editScope=ThisAndFollowing` | 200 | `true` (series split from occurrence) |
| B2-4 | Recurring, `editScope=All` | 200 | `true` (entire series) |
| B2-5 | Invalid fields (same as B1) | 400 | Matching validation message |
| B2-6 | Recurring, `editScope != All`, missing `occurrenceDate` | 400 | Occurrence date required for series edit |
| B2-7 | Event missing/deleted | 404 | Event not found |
| B2-8 | User not creator/authorized | 403 | Not allowed to edit this event |
| B2-9 | Time change causes invitee conflict | 409 | User {name} is in a meeting in that time range |

---

## B3. DELETE `/Calendar/Event`

**Valid input (query):** `id:int` + `editScope` + `occurrenceDate?` (like B2).  
**Behavior:** soft delete; for recurring, per `editScope`.  
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| B3-1 | Non-recurring, valid delete | 200 | `true` |
| B3-2 | Recurring `ThisOnly` | 200 | `true` (IsCancelled exception) |
| B3-3 | Recurring `ThisAndFollowing` | 200 | `true` |
| B3-4 | Recurring `All` | 200 | `true` |
| B3-5 | `editScope != All` and missing `occurrenceDate` | 400 | Occurrence date required |
| B3-6 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| B3-7 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to delete this event |

---

## B5. GET `/Calendar/Range`

**Valid input (query):**
| Parameter | Rule |
|---|---|
| `from` | Required |
| `to` | Required, `> from`, span ≤ 92 days |
| `calendarIds` | Optional CSV; if empty → all visible calendars |
| `includeTaskDeadlines` | Optional, default `true` |

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  {
    "kind": "Event", "eventId": 501, "taskId": null,
    "title": "Project review meeting", "type": "Meeting",
    "startAt": "2026-07-09T05:30:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-09T06:30:00Z",
    "isAllDay": false, "status": "Confirmed", "color": "#FF9800", "occurrenceDate": null
  },
  {
    "kind": "Event", "eventId": 502, "taskId": null,
    "title": "Weekly standup", "type": "Meeting",
    "startAt": "2026-07-13T05:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-13T06:00:00Z",
    "isAllDay": false, "status": "Confirmed", "color": "#FF9800",
    "occurrenceDate": "2026-07-13T00:00:00Z"
  },
  {
    "kind": "TaskDeadline", "eventId": null, "taskId": 88,
    "title": "Due: deliver report", "type": "Deadline",
    "startAt": "2026-07-10T20:30:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-10T20:30:00Z",
    "isAllDay": false, "status": null, "color": "#000000", "occurrenceDate": null
  }
]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| B5-1 | Valid range, no data | 200 | `[]` |
| B5-2 | Valid range with events and task deadlines | 200 | Combined array (Event + TaskDeadline) |
| B5-3 | `includeTaskDeadlines=false` | 200 | Events only |
| B5-4 | Recurring event in range | 200 | Multiple items with `occurrenceDate` set |
| B5-5 | `to <= from` | 400 | Invalid time range |
| B5-6 | Range > 92 days | 400 | Maximum allowed range is 92 days |
| B5-7 | Missing/invalid `from` or `to` | 400 | Invalid date |

## B6. GET `/Calendar/Subordinates/Range`

**Valid input (query):** `from`, `to` (like B5; ≤ 92 days).  
**Logic:** subordinates from `OrgPosition` tree (`ManagerId`/`ParentId`).  
**Privacy:** normal subordinate events full detail; **personal** events busy-only without title.

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  { "userId": 55, "userFullName": "Maryam Rezaei", "startAt": "2026-07-09T05:30:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-09T06:30:00Z", "title": "Project review meeting", "isBusyOnly": false },
  { "userId": 55, "userFullName": "Maryam Rezaei", "startAt": "2026-07-09T08:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-09T09:00:00Z", "title": null, "isBusyOnly": true }
]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| B6-1 | Manager with subordinates | 200 | Array (personal = busy only) |
| B6-2 | User with no subordinates | 200 | `[]` |
| B6-3 | Invalid range / > 92 days | 400 | Matching message |

# Group E — Meeting request

## E1. POST `/Calendar/MeetingRequest`

**Valid input — `MeetingRequestCreateDto`:**
| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `targetUserId` | Required; valid user; ≠ requester |
| `title` | Required, 2..200 |
| `description` | Optional |
| `location` | Optional |
| `startAt` | Required |
| `endAt` | Required, `> startAt` |

**Logic:** slot must be free for target; in-app notification to target.  
**200 output:** `int` (request id). Example: `301`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| E1-1 | Valid request on free slot | 200 | `301` (+ notification to target) |
| E1-2 | Invalid `title` | 400 | Validation message |
| E1-3 | `endAt <= startAt` | 400 | End time must be after start |
| E1-4 | `targetUserId == self` | 400 | Cannot request meeting with yourself |
| E1-5 | `targetUserId` not found | 404 | User not found |
| E1-6 | Target busy in range | 409 | User {name} is in a meeting in that time range |

---

## E2. POST `/Calendar/MeetingRequest/Approve`

**Valid input:** `{ requestId:int }`.  
**Logic:** creates `Confirmed` event; both users invited; reminders set; notification to requester.  
**200 output:** `int` (created event id). Example: `510`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| E2-1 | Valid approve by target | 200 | `510` (event + reminders + notification) |
| E2-2 | Request not found | 404 | Meeting request not found |
| E2-3 | Approver is not target | 403 | Only target can approve |
| E2-4 | Request already processed | 400 | Request already processed |
| E2-5 | Target slot now conflicted | 409 | Another meeting exists in this slot |

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| E3-1 | Valid reject by target | 200 | `true` (+ notification) |
| E3-2 | Request not found | 404 | Meeting request not found |
| E3-3 | User is not target | 403 | Only target can reject |
| E3-4 | Request already processed | 400 | Request already processed |

## E7. POST `/Calendar/MeetingRequest/Cancel`

**Valid input:** `{ requestId:int }`.  
**Logic:** requester only, only while `Pending`; optional notification to target.  
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| E7-1 | Valid cancel by requester | 200 | `true` |
| E7-2 | Request not found | 404 | Meeting request not found |
| E7-3 | User is not requester | 403 | Only requester can cancel |
| E7-4 | Request not Pending | 400 | Request cannot be cancelled |

---

# Group F — Reminders

## F1. POST `/Calendar/Event/Reminder`

**Valid input:** `{ eventId:int, offsetMinutes:int(>=0), channel:"InApp"|"Sms" }`.  
**200 output:** `int` (reminder id). Example: `7`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1-1 | Valid add | 200 | `7` |
| F1-2 | Negative `offsetMinutes` | 400 | Invalid reminder offset |
| F1-3 | Invalid `channel` | 400 | Invalid reminder channel |
| F1-4 | `channel=Sms` but user has no phone | 400 | Phone number required for SMS reminder |
| F1-5 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| F1-6 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to add reminder |
| F1-7 | Duplicate (same offset + channel) | 400 | Reminder already exists |

---

## F2. DELETE `/Calendar/Event/Reminder/{id}`

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| F2-1 | Valid delete | 200 | `true` |
| F2-2 | Reminder not found | 404 | Reminder not found |
| F2-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to delete reminder |

---

## F3. GET `/Calendar/Event/{id}/Reminders`

**200 sample:**
```json
[ { "id": 7, "offsetMinutes": 15, "channel": "Sms", "isSent": false } ]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| F3-1 | Event exists | 200 | `ReminderDto` array |
| F3-2 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| F3-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to view |

> Background (non-API): `ReminderBackgroundService` periodically sends due reminders (`StartAt - OffsetMinutes <= now`, `IsSent=false`): in-app + SMS; then sets `IsSent=true` and `SentAt`.

---

# Group G — Attachments & notes

## G1. POST `/Calendar/Event/Attachment` (multipart/form-data)

**Valid input:** form with `eventId:int` + `file`.  
**File rules:** max **10 MB**; allowed: image (jpg/png), PDF, Word (doc/docx).

**200 sample:**
```json
{ "id": 21, "fileName": "agenda.pdf", "fileUrl": "/files/calendar/510/agenda.pdf", "sizeBytes": 234112, "uploadedAt": "2026-07-08T12:10:00Z" }
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G1-1 | Valid upload | 200 | `AttachmentDto` |
| G1-2 | Missing/empty file | 400 | File required |
| G1-3 | Size > 10 MB | 400 | File size must not exceed 10 MB |
| G1-4 | Disallowed type | 400 | File type not allowed |
| G1-5 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| G1-6 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to add attachment |

---

## G2. DELETE `/Calendar/Event/Attachment/{id}`

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G2-1 | Valid delete | 200 | `true` |
| G2-2 | Attachment not found | 404 | Attachment not found |
| G2-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to delete attachment |

---

## G3. GET `/Calendar/Event/{id}/Attachments`

**200 output:** `AttachmentDto[]`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G3-1 | Event exists | 200 | Array (may be empty) |
| G3-2 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| G3-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to view |

---

## G4. POST `/Calendar/Event/Note`

**Valid input:** `{ eventId:int, noteId:int?, text:string }`.  
- Empty `noteId` → create; set `noteId` → update that note.  
- `text` required, non-empty.  
**200 output:** `int` (note id).

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G4-1 | Valid create | 200 | Note id |
| G4-2 | Valid update (author only) | 200 | Same note id |
| G4-3 | Empty `text` | 400 | Note text required |
| G4-4 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| G4-5 | `noteId` set but not found | 404 | Note not found |
| G4-6 | Edit note owned by another user | 403 | Not allowed to edit this note |

---

## G5. GET `/Calendar/Event/{id}/Notes`

**200 sample:**
```json
[ { "id": 4, "text": "Please prepare the report before the meeting", "authorUserId": 42, "createdAt": "2026-07-08T12:15:00Z" } ]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G5-1 | Event exists | 200 | `NoteDto` array |
| G5-2 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| G5-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to view |

---

## G6. DELETE `/Calendar/Event/Note/{id}`

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| G6-1 | Valid delete (author or event creator) | 200 | `true` |
| G6-2 | Note not found | 404 | Note not found |
| G6-3 | User not authorized | 403 | Not allowed to delete this note |

---

# Appendix: Global recurring rules

These apply to **all** endpoints and are not repeated in each table:

| Code | Global condition | Expected output |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | Missing/expired/invalid token | No body |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded | No body (may include `Retry-After`) |
| 400 | Unexpected handler exception | ProblemDetails with `Error.ServerError` |

## Conflict rule (409)
On **B1, B2, D1, E1, E2**: if a user who would be committed to a time range already has a `Confirmed` event in that range, operation **fails** with `409` and message “User {name} is in a meeting in that time range”.

## Privacy rule (view)
- `visibleOnlyToParticipants=true`: only creator and invitees see details (others get 403 on B4).
- `isPersonal=true`: Free/Busy and subordinate view show busy slot only, no title.

---

# Group H — Favorite contacts + holidays (meeting backlog)

> From meeting notes: quick invite UX and company holiday display.

## H1. GET `/Calendar/Favorites`

**Valid input:** none.

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  { "id": 10, "targetUserId": 55, "targetFullName": "Maryam Rezaei" },
  { "id": 11, "targetUserId": 60, "targetFullName": "Hossein Ahmadi" }
]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| H1-1 | List exists | 200 | Array (may be empty) |

## H2. POST `/Calendar/Favorites`

**Valid input:** `{ targetUserId:int }` (≠ current user).  
**200 output:** `int` (favorite id). Example: `11`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| H2-1 | Valid add | 200 | `11` |
| H2-2 | `targetUserId` not found | 404 | User not found |
| H2-3 | `targetUserId` is self | 400 | Cannot favorite yourself |
| H2-4 | Already favorited | 200 | Existing id (duplicate ignored) |

## H3. DELETE `/Calendar/Favorites/{id}`

**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| H3-1 | Valid delete | 200 | `true` |
| H3-2 | Not found | 404 | Favorite contact not found |
| H3-3 | Owned by another user | 403 | Not allowed to delete |

## H4. GET `/Calendar/Holidays/Range?from=&to=&scope=&scopeId=`

**Valid input:**
- `from`, `to` like B5 (≤ 92 days)
- `scope` one of `Organization|Unit|Project`
- `scopeId` required for Unit/Project

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  { "id": 1, "title": "Public holiday", "startAt": "2026-07-15T00:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-15T23:59:59Z", "isAllDay": true }
]
```

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| H4-1 | Valid range | 200 | Array (may be empty) |
| H4-2 | Invalid range / > 92 days | 400 | Matching message |
| H4-3 | Invalid `scope` | 400 | Invalid scope |
| H4-4 | `scope=Unit` and missing `scopeId` | 400 | Unit id required |

---

# Group I — Smart Slot Finder + meeting buffer (meeting backlog)

## I1. GET `/Calendar/SmartSlots?userIds=&from=&to=&durationMinutes=`

**Valid input:**
- `userIds` CSV, min 1
- `from`, `to` valid (≤ 92 days)
- `durationMinutes` required, min 5, max 480

**200 sample:**
```json
[
  { "startAt": "2026-07-11T06:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-11T06:30:00Z", "score": 0.92 },
  { "startAt": "2026-07-11T07:00:00Z", "endAt": "2026-07-11T07:30:00Z", "score": 0.81 }
]
```
> `score` = quality (free participants + low conflict + buffer).

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| I1-1 | Valid input | 200 | Suggestion list (may be empty) |
| I1-2 | Invalid `durationMinutes` | 400 | Invalid meeting duration |
| I1-3 | Invalid range | 400 | Matching message |
| I1-4 | Invalid user id | 400 | Invalid user in list |

## I2. PUT `/Calendar/Preferences/Buffer`

**Valid input:** `{ defaultBufferMinutes:int }` (0..30).  
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| I2-1 | Valid save | 200 | `true` |
| I2-2 | Value out of range | 400 | Buffer must be 0..30 minutes |

---

# Group J — Room/resource booking (meeting backlog)

## J1. GET `/Calendar/Resources`
**200 output:** `CalendarResourceDto[]`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| J1-1 | Resource list | 200 | Array (may be empty) |

## J2. POST `/Calendar/Resources` (admin)
**Valid input:** `{ title:string(2..200), type:"Room"|"Equipment"|"Other" }`  
**200 output:** `int`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |

| J2-1 | Valid create | 200 | Resource id |
| J2-2 | Invalid data | 400 | Validation message |
| J2-3 | Insufficient permission | 403 | Not allowed |

## J3. PUT `/Calendar/Resources` (admin)
**Valid input:** `{ id:int, title:string, type:..., isActive:bool }`  
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| J3-1 | Valid update | 200 | `true` |
| J3-2 | Not found | 404 | Resource not found |
| J3-3 | Insufficient permission | 403 | Not allowed |

## J4. DELETE `/Calendar/Resources/{id}` (admin)
**200 output:** `true`

| # | Condition | Code | Expected output |
|---|---|---|---|
| J4-1 | Valid soft delete | 200 | `true` |
| J4-2 | Not found | 404 | Resource not found |
| J4-3 | Insufficient permission | 403 | Not allowed |

## J5. POST `/Calendar/Event/Resources`

**Valid input:** `{ eventId:int, resourceIds:int[] }`  
**200 output:** `true`

| J5-2 | Event not found | 404 | Event not found |
| J5-3 | Resource not found | 404 | Resource not found |
| J5-4 | Resource conflict | 409 | Resource already booked in this range |

---

# Group K — Scheduling links + guests (meeting backlog / optional)

## K1. POST `/Calendar/SchedulingLinks`

input.contents:
# Enterprise Calendar, Meeting Minutes & Task — Frontend Implementation Plan

> **Audience:** Mobile / Web frontend developers and Cursor agents  
> **Stack (recommended):** Flutter (same as Chat module) — patterns below also map to any HTTP client  
> **Backend:** `Customer/Api` (REST + JWT)  
> **Base URL (dev):** `https://localhost:8181`  
> **Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` on every request  
> **Success HTTP status:** Always `200 OK` (project convention — never expect `201 Created`)

This document tells **what to build**, **in what order**, and **which backend contract to call**. It does **not** duplicate every request/response shape — use the linked backend docs as the source of truth for DTO fields and error scenarios.

---

## How to use this document (Cursor agent)

1. Read **Global conventions** once before any module work.
2. Implement **one phase at a time**; do not skip Phase 0 (HTTP/auth/date helpers).
3. For Calendar endpoints, cross-check every API call against:
   - `Customer/Api/Docs/Calendar/01-Calendar-Overview.md` (design + API list)
   - `Customer/Api/Docs/Calendar/02-Calendar-Scenarios.md` (status codes + edge cases)
4. For Minutes/Task scope and integration, read `Customer/Api/Docs/Elay-Feature-Spec-v1.md`.
5. For **existing** Task APIs, read `Customer/Api/Docs/Task.md`.
6. Mirror the Chat module’s client architecture (`docs/CHAT_FRONTEND_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` Phase 1): shared `ApiClient`, token refresh, 429 handling, secure storage.
7. **Do not build UI for backlog APIs** (groups H–K in Calendar overview) until product confirms and backend ships them.

---

## Backend reference map

| Topic | Document |
|---|---|
| Three-module requirements & integration | `Customer/Api/Docs/Elay-Feature-Spec-v1.md` |
| Calendar design, entities, phasing, API index | `Customer/Api/Docs/Calendar/01-Calendar-Overview.md` |
| Calendar per-endpoint scenarios | `Customer/Api/Docs/Calendar/02-Calendar-Scenarios.md` |
| Existing Task endpoints | `Customer/Api/Docs/Task.md` |
| In-app notifications (meeting request, reminders) | `Customer/Api/Docs/Notification.md` |
| Auth / token refresh | `Customer/Api/Docs/Auth.md` |
| Chat client foundation (reuse patterns) | `docs/CHAT_FRONTEND_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` |

**Backend status (important):** Calendar and Meeting Minutes APIs are **designed but not implemented yet**. Task APIs **partially exist**. Phases below mark each API group as `READY` (exists today) or `PLANNED` (wait for backend).

---

## Table of contents

- [Global conventions](#global-conventions)
- [Recommended packages](#recommended-packages)
- [Shared client layer](#shared-client-layer)
- [Visual & UX rules](#visual--ux-rules)
- [Phase 0 — Foundation](#phase-0--foundation)
- [Module A — Enterprise Calendar](#module-a--enterprise-calendar)
- [Module B — Meeting Minutes](#module-b--meeting-minutes)
- [Module C — Task Management](#module-c--task-management)
- [Cross-module flows](#cross-module-flows)
- [Error handling cheat sheet](#error-handling-cheat-sheet)
- [Agent definition-of-done (full V1)](#agent-definition-of-done-full-v1)

---

## Global conventions

### Authentication & HTTP

- Attach JWT on **every** call via a central interceptor (same as Chat).
- On `401`: try refresh once, then force logout.
- On `429`: show “too many requests”; optional retry with backoff.
- All JSON APIs return success as **`200`** with the raw body (no `{ isSuccess, data }` wrapper at HTTP level).

### Dates & locale

| Layer | Rule |
|---|---|
| API request/response | ISO 8601 UTC (`DateTime`) — e.g. `2026-07-09T05:30:00Z` |
| UI display | **Jalali (Shamsi)** calendar + Persian labels |
| Conversion | Client-side only — never send Jalali strings to API |
| All-day events | Send UTC midnight boundaries per backend rules; UI shows full-day chip |

Use `intl` + a Jalali date package (e.g. `shamsi_date` or `persian_datetime_picker` in Flutter).

### Pagination

- Query param `PageIndex` is **zero-based** (0 = first page).
- Display `currentPageNumber = pageIndex + 1` in UI.
- Calendar range queries use `from` / `to` (max **92 days** per request).

### RTL

- App is **RTL** (Persian). Mirror layouts; keep icons that imply direction consistent with RTL.

### Privacy rules (must enforce in UI)

| Rule | UI behavior |
|---|---|
| Free/Busy | Show colored “busy” blocks only — **never** title/description |
| Personal events (`isPersonal=true`) | Same: busy block without title for other users |
| `visibleOnlyToParticipants=true` | Hide event details from non-participants (403 from API) |
| Manager subordinate view | Full title for normal events; personal → busy-only |

### Event type colors (V1)

Map `EventType` to fixed theme tokens (match product icons):

| Type | Suggested color | Icon hint |
|---|---|---|
| `Urgent` | Red | Alert |
| `Private` | Grey | Lock |
| `Confirmed` | Green | Check |
| `Meeting` | Orange | People |
| `Info` | Blue | Info |
| `Deadline` | Black/dark | Clock |
| `TaskDeadline` (range item) | Black/dark | Task/checklist (read-only) |

---

## Recommended packages

Reuse the Chat module stack where possible:

| Concern | Flutter package | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| HTTP | `dio` | Interceptors for JWT, 401 refresh, 429 |
| Secure storage | `flutter_secure_storage` | Tokens |
| State | `flutter_bloc` or `riverpod` | Pick one; calendar benefits from shared range cache |
| Jalali dates | `shamsi_date` + `persian_datetime_picker` | Display + pickers |
| File pick/upload | `file_picker` | Attachments ≤ 10 MB |
| Image preview | `cached_network_image` / `photo_view` | Attachment list |
| Connectivity | `connectivity_plus` | Offline banner on calendar range |

---

## Shared client layer

Create **`CalendarApiService`** (and later `MinutesApiService`, extend existing `TaskApiService`) on top of the shared `ApiClient`.

### Suggested Dart models (mirror backend DTO names)

```dart
enum EventType { urgent, private, confirmed, meeting, info, deadline }
enum EventStatus { pending, confirmed, rejected, cancelled }
enum ParticipantResponse { pending, accepted, declined, maybe }
enum RecurrenceEditScope { thisOnly, thisAndFollowing, all }
enum ReminderChannel { inApp, sms }

class CalendarDto { /* id, title, type, ownerUserId, orgPositionId, color, canWrite */ }
class RangeItemDto { /* kind: Event|TaskDeadline, eventId?, taskId?, title, startAt, endAt, type, color, occurrenceDate? */ }
class EventDetailDto { /* full event + participants + reminders + recurrence + attachments + notes */ }
class MeetingRequestDto { /* id, requester, target, title, startAt, endAt, status */ }
class FreeBusyDto { /* userId, busySlots[] */ }
```

Generate from OpenAPI when available; until then, copy fields from `02-Calendar-Scenarios.md` samples.

### Range cache strategy

- Cache `GET /Calendar/Range` results keyed by `(from, to, calendarIds)`.
- Invalidate cache on: event create/update/delete, RSVP change, meeting request approve/reject.
- Prefetch adjacent months when user swipes calendar (stay within 92-day window per request; split wider views into multiple API calls).

---

## Visual & UX rules

### Navigation (suggested routes)

| Route | Screen |
|---|---|
| `/calendar` | Main calendar (month/week/day toggle) |
| `/calendar/event/:id` | Event detail |
| `/calendar/meeting-requests` | Pending + sent requests |
| `/calendar/subordinates` | Manager busy overview |
| `/minutes/:eventId` | Meeting minutes editor |
| `/tasks` | Task list (To Do / Done) |
| `/tasks/:id` | Task detail / edit |

### Reusable widgets to build

| Widget | Used in |
|---|---|
| `JalaliCalendarGrid` | Main calendar |
| `EventChip` | Range items on grid |
| `BusyBlock` | Free/Busy + subordinate views (no title) |
| `UserMultiSelectSheet` | Invites, meeting request target |
| `RecurrenceEditor` | Event create/edit |
| `RecurrenceScopeDialog` | Edit/delete recurring occurrence |
| `ReminderListEditor` | Offsets + InApp/Sms channels |
| `MeetingRequestCard` | Pending/sent lists |
| `AttachmentUploader` | Multipart ≤ 10 MB |
| `FavoriteContactsRow` | Quick invite (backlog H) |

### Empty & loading states (Persian copy)

| Screen | Empty message |
|---|---|
| Calendar range | «رویدادی در این بازه ثبت نشده است» |
| Meeting requests | «درخواست جلسه‌ای وجود ندارد» |
| Tasks To Do | «تسکی برای انجام ندارید» |
| Minutes | «برای این جلسه صورت‌جلسه‌ای ثبت نشده است» |

---

## Phase 0 — Foundation

**Goal:** Auth + HTTP + Jalali helpers — no feature UI yet.

**Backend:** `Auth.md` — `READY`

**Build:**

- Reuse Chat `ApiClient`, token storage, refresh flow.
- `DateTimeUtils`: UTC ↔ Jalali display, range clamp (max 92 days).
- `ProblemDetailsParser`: read `detail` for snackbar/toast (Persian text from API).
- `NotificationBadgeService`: poll or push hook for `GET /Notification` (for meeting request alerts).

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Authenticated GET to `/Calendar/List` compiles and runs (even if backend returns 404 until implemented — handle gracefully).
- [ ] Jalali date picker returns UTC `DateTime` for API payloads.
- [ ] 409 conflict shows user-friendly Persian message from `detail`.

---

## Module A — Enterprise Calendar

Implement in order. API groups reference `01-Calendar-Overview.md` §7.

### A1 — Calendar list & shell (`PLANNED`: A1–A4)

**APIs:** `GET /Calendar/List`, `POST /Calendar`, `PUT /Calendar`, `DELETE /Calendar/{id}`

**Screens:**

- **`CalendarShellScreen`**
  - Side drawer or filter chips: Personal / Unit / Organization calendars (multi-select).
  - On first open: `GET /Calendar/List` — personal calendar auto-created by backend.
  - Toggle visibility per calendar (client state); pass `calendarIds` to range query.

**UI notes:**

- Default personal calendar cannot be deleted (expect 400 — disable delete in UI).
- Unit calendar create: require org position picker (reuse user/org pickers from Chat user list patterns).

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] User sees at least personal calendar after login.
- [ ] Can create unit calendar with position id.
- [ ] Calendar colors apply to event chips on grid.

---

### A2 — Range view & main grid (`PLANNED`: B5, B6)

**APIs:**

- `GET /Calendar/Range?from=&to=&calendarIds=&includeTaskDeadlines=`
- `GET /Calendar/Subordinates/Range?from=&to=` (manager only)

**Screens:**

- **`CalendarMainScreen`**
  - Month / week / day views driven by **one range query** per visible window.
  - Render `RangeItemDto`:
    - `kind == Event` → normal chip with title + color by `type`
    - `kind == TaskDeadline` → read-only deadline chip (no navigation to edit task from calendar in V1 unless product asks — tap can deep-link to task detail)
  - Recurring instances: same `eventId`, different `occurrenceDate` — show each occurrence separately.
  - Enforce 92-day max: if user navigates wider, split into parallel requests and merge.

- **`SubordinatesBusyScreen`** (managers)
  - Timeline of subordinate slots.
  - If `isBusyOnly == true` or `title == null` → render `BusyBlock` without label.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Month view loads events + task deadlines for selected calendars.
- [ ] Tapping event opens detail (A4).
- [ ] Task deadlines visible, not editable from calendar.
- [ ] Manager sees subordinate busy slots; personal events hide title.

---

### A3 — Event CRUD (`PLANNED`: B1–B4, B2/B3 recurrence scope)

**APIs:** `POST/PUT/DELETE /Calendar/Event`, `GET /Calendar/Event/{id}`

**Screens:**

- **`EventDetailScreen`**
  - Full `EventDetailDto`: participants, reminders, attachments, notes, recurrence summary.
  - Actions: Edit, Delete, Respond (if invitee), Open Minutes (Module B link when event type is Meeting).

- **`EventEditScreen`** (create + edit)
  - Fields: title, description, location, type, start/end, all-day, personal, visible-only-to-participants.
  - Participant multi-select (`UsersSelect` pattern).
  - Recurrence editor (B-Recur rules from scenarios doc).
  - On save conflict **409**: show «{name} در آن بازه در جلسه است» from `detail`.

- **`RecurrenceScopeDialog`**
  - Shown on edit/delete recurring event: This only / This and following / All.
  - Sends `editScope` + `occurrenceDate` query/body per backend contract.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Create non-recurring event refreshes range cache.
- [ ] Edit single occurrence of series without breaking whole series.
- [ ] 409 blocks save with visible error.
- [ ] Participant-only events hidden from unauthorized users.

---

### A4 — Free/Busy & scheduling assistant (`PLANNED`: C1)

**API:** `GET /Calendar/FreeBusy?userIds=&from=&to=`

**Screens / widgets:**

- **`FreeBusyPickerSheet`**
  - Used inside Event create and Meeting request flows.
  - Shows merged busy timeline for selected users (no titles).
  - User picks a free slot → pre-fills start/end on form.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Selecting 3 users shows combined busy blocks.
- [ ] Cannot pick overlapping busy slot (validate client-side + trust 409 from server).

---

### A5 — Invites & RSVP (`PLANNED`: D1–D4)

**APIs:** Participant add/remove/respond/list.

**UI:**

- On `EventDetailScreen`: participant list with response badges (Pending / Accepted / Declined / Maybe).
- Invite action opens `UserMultiSelectSheet` (favorites first when backlog H ships).
- Push/in-app notification → deep link to event detail for RSVP.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Invitee can Accept/Decline/Maybe.
- [ ] Organizer can add/remove participants.
- [ ] 409 when inviting busy user.

---

### A6 — Meeting request flow (`PLANNED`: E1–E7)

**APIs:** Create, approve, reject, pending, sent, get by id, cancel.

**Screens:**

- **`MeetingRequestCreateScreen`**
  - Pick target user → Free/Busy picker → title/description/location → submit `POST /Calendar/MeetingRequest`.

- **`MeetingRequestsScreen`** (tabs: Received / Sent)
  - `GET .../Pending` and `GET .../Sent` with pagination.
  - Received: Approve / Reject buttons.
  - Sent: Cancel while `Pending` (E7).

- **`MeetingRequestDetailScreen`**
  - Read-only summary before approve/reject.

**Notifications:**

- Subscribe to notification center entries for new requests, approve/reject outcomes.
- Approve success returns new `eventId` — navigate to event detail.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] End-to-end: request → approve → both users see confirmed event on calendar.
- [ ] Reject notifies requester (via notification list).
- [ ] Requester can cancel pending request.
- [ ] 409 if slot taken before approve.

---

### A7 — Reminders (`PLANNED`: F1–F3)

**APIs:** Add/remove/list reminders on event.

**UI:**

- Reminder list on event edit/detail: offset minutes + channel (InApp / Sms).
- Validate SMS channel requires user phone (400 from API).
- Default UX copy: «۱۵ دقیقه قبل» for confirmed meetings (backend may auto-create — show but allow edit).

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] User can add InApp + Sms reminders.
- [ ] Duplicate offset+channel shows error.

---

### A8 — Attachments & notes (`PLANNED`: G1–G6)

**APIs:** Multipart upload, list, delete attachments; CRUD notes.

**UI:**

- `AttachmentUploader`: max 10 MB; types image/pdf/doc — validate before upload.
- Notes list: author + timestamp; edit own notes only.

**Definition of done:**

- [ ] Upload PDF shows in list with download/open.
- [ ] Oversize file blocked client-side with Persian message.

---

### A9 — Calendar backlog (do NOT build until backend + product sign-off)

Groups **H–K** from overview: favorites, holidays, smart slots, resources, scheduling links, guests. Keep feature flags off.

---

## Module B — Meeting Minutes

**Backend status:** `PLANNED` (Phase 7 in `Elay-Feature-Spec-v1.md`).  
Build UI against **planned** REST shape below; adjust when backend ships OpenAPI.

### Planned API surface (from requirements spec)

| Action | Suggested route | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Get minutes by event | `GET /Minutes/{eventId}` | 404 if not created yet |
| Create/update minutes | `POST /Minutes` | Upsert linked to `CalendarEvent` |
| Agenda items CRUD | `POST/PUT/DELETE /Minutes/Agenda` | Ordered list |
| Decisions CRUD | `POST/PUT/DELETE /Minutes/Decision` | |
| Action items | `POST/PUT/DELETE /Minutes/Action` | Assignee + due date |
| Action accept/reject | `POST /Minutes/Action/Respond` | Assignee workflow |
| Action status update | `POST /Minutes/Action/Status` | Done / not done + reason |
| Participants | `POST/DELETE /Minutes/Participant` | Internal + guest mobile |
| Personal note | `POST /Minutes/PersonalNote` | Private per user |
| Approvals | `POST /Minutes/Approval/Request`, `POST /Minutes/Approval/Respond` | Digital sign-off |
| PDF export | `GET /Minutes/{id}/Pdf` | V2 backlog |

### M1 — Minutes entry point

**Screen:** `EventDetailScreen` → button «صورت‌جلسه» visible when:

- Event `type == Meeting` (or Confirmed meeting)
- Current user is participant or organizer

Load `GET /Minutes/{eventId}`; if 404, show «ایجاد صورت‌جلسه».

---

### M2 — Minutes editor layout

**Screen:** `MinutesEditorScreen` (multi-section form)

| Section | Fields |
|---|---|
| Header | Event title, date (Jalali), location (read-only from event) |
| Attendees | Internal users + guest name/phone rows |
| Agenda | Ordered items, add/remove/reorder |
| Discussion | Rich text or bullet list per topic |
| Decisions | List of decision records |
| Outcome | Overall result summary |
| Action items | Table: assignee, description, due date, status |
| My private note | Visible only to current user |

Auto-save draft locally; explicit Save calls `POST /Minutes`.

---

### M3 — Action item → Task handoff

When assignee **accepts** action (`POST /Minutes/Action/Respond`):

- Backend creates task in assignee’s list (Task module).
- Frontend: toast «تسک به لیست شما اضافه شد» + link to `/tasks/:id`.

When assignee marks task done in Task module:

- Minutes action row should refresh status (`Minutes/Action/Status` sync).

---

### M4 — Approvals

- Organizer sends approval request to selected participants.
- Each participant sees pending approval on minutes header.
- Show per-user approval status chips.

---

### M5 — Guest / SMS (optional V1)

- Guest row: name + mobile.
- SMS summary on publish — UI shows «ارسال پیامک به مهمان» only if backend confirms SMS module ready.

---

## Module C — Task Management

**Backend status:** **Partial** — see `Customer/Api/Docs/Task.md` (`READY` for basic CRUD/list).

### Existing APIs to integrate first (`READY`)

| Method | Route | Use |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/Task?page=` | Task list (paginated) |
| GET | `/Task/{id}` | Task detail/edit form |
| POST | `/Task` | Create task |

Verify exact DTO field names in Swagger when backend running.

### T1 — Task list shell

**Screen:** `TaskListScreen`

- Tabs or columns: **To Do** / **Done** (filter client-side until backend adds status filter).
- Pagination: `page` query (zero-based — confirm against live API).
- Pull-to-refresh.

**Planned enhancements (`PLANNED`):** folders, personal vs org space, tags, priority — UI slots ready but hidden behind feature flag.

---

### T2 — Task create/edit

**Screen:** `TaskEditScreen`

- Fields: title, description, start/end, due date (Jalali pickers → UTC API).
- Reminder toggle (when API exists).
- Link to source: `minutesActionId` / `eventId` read-only when created from Minutes.

---

### T3 — Calendar integration (read-only)

- Already covered in **A2**: show `TaskDeadline` on calendar range.
- Tap deadline chip → navigate to `TaskEditScreen` (read-only or full edit per product).

---

### T4 — Minutes integration

- From task detail: if linked to minutes action, show banner «مربوط به صورت‌جلسه» + link back.
- Completing task prompts for completion note if minutes action requires reason when not done on time.

---

### T5 — Quick add from calendar (`PLANNED`)

- Long-press on calendar slot → «ایجاد تسک» with pre-filled due date/time.

---

## Cross-module flows

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph Calendar
    E[Meeting Event]
    MR[Meeting Request]
  end
  subgraph Minutes
    M[Minutes Form]
    A[Action Items]
  end
  subgraph Task
    T[Task List]
  end
  MR -->|approve| E
  E -->|open| M
  M -->|assign| A
  A -->|accept| T
  T -->|due date| E
  T -->|status sync| A
```

| User journey | Steps |
|---|---|
| Schedule meeting | Free/Busy → create event OR meeting request → approve |
| Run meeting | Open event → Minutes → fill agenda/decisions |
| Assign work | Action items → assignee notification → accept → task appears |
| Track on calendar | Task due dates on range view |
| Manager oversight | Subordinates busy view + task/minutes status (future analytics V3) |

---

## Error handling cheat sheet

| HTTP | Meaning | UI action |
|---|---|---|
| 200 | Success | Update cache + navigate back |
| 400 | Validation / business rule | Show `ProblemDetails.detail` (Persian) |
| 401 | Unauthorized | Refresh token or logout |
| 403 | Forbidden | Toast + disable action |
| 404 | Not found | Empty state or back navigation |
| 409 | Time conflict / resource busy | Inline error on time picker; keep form open |
| 429 | Rate limit | Retry message |

**Validation quirk:** Some validation failures may return HTTP 200 with error body in older endpoints — Calendar V1 uses `ProblemDetails` on 400/403/404/409 per scenarios doc. Prefer checking status code first.

---

## Agent definition-of-done (full V1)

### Calendar

- [ ] Month/week/day views with Jalali labels
- [ ] Event CRUD + recurrence scopes
- [ ] Free/Busy picker + meeting request lifecycle
- [ ] RSVP + participant management
- [ ] Reminders UI (InApp + Sms)
- [ ] Attachments + notes
- [ ] Task deadlines on calendar (read-only)
- [ ] Manager subordinate busy view
- [ ] Notifications deep-link to requests/events

### Meeting Minutes

- [ ] Minutes editor linked to meeting event
- [ ] Agenda, decisions, action items
- [ ] Assignee accept/reject → task creation
- [ ] Status sync back from tasks
- [ ] Private note per participant

### Task

- [ ] List + detail using existing Task API
- [ ] Due date visible on calendar
- [ ] Link from minutes-derived tasks

### Non-functional

- [ ] RTL Persian UI throughout
- [ ] UTC API / Jalali display tested across timezones
- [ ] Optimistic UI with rollback on 400/409
- [ ] No implementation of backlog groups H–K without backend
